documents.title: UK Student Route Visa — Official Requirements
documents.summary: The Student Route (formerly Tier 4) is the primary visa for international students at UK universities. It requires a Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS) from a UKVI-licensed sponsor and proof of financial maintenance. From July 2025, visa decisions are issued as a digital eVisa through a UKVI account rather than a physical BRP sticker.

scope.failureImpact: Visa refusal, curtailment of leave, removal from UK, scholarship revocation

timeline.T-6 months: Earliest application date before course start
timeline.Within 7 days (arrival): Police registration if visa requires it; address registration with sponsor

details: You must have a valid CAS reference number from a UKVI-licensed Student sponsor before applying
CAS contains your course details, tuition fees paid, and sponsor's licence number
CAS is valid for 6 months — apply within this window
One CAS per application; changing institution after visa grant requires a new visa in most cases
Check your sponsor appears on the UKVI Register of Licensed Sponsors with 'track record' status, which affects your work rights

commonViolations.text: Beginning studies before visa is granted
commonViolations.consequence: Working or studying without leave — status violation, removal risk

commonViolations.text: Changing to a different course or institution without a new visa
commonViolations.consequence: Breach of visa conditions, curtailment of leave

controls: Confirm CAS reference number before applying
Verify your sponsor's 'track record' status on the UKVI register (affects work hour limit)
Apply no earlier than 6 months before your course start date

details: London institutions: £1,483 per month for up to 9 months (maximum £13,347)
Outside London: £1,136 per month for up to 9 months (maximum £10,224)
Funds must have been held for at least 28 consecutive days
The 28-day period must end within 31 days of your visa application date
If your CAS shows tuition fees already paid, you may not need to show the full amount
MoHE sponsorship letters are accepted as evidence of financial support
failureModes: Funds held for fewer than 28 days
28-day period ending more than 31 days before application
Using joint accounts or funds not in your name without declaration
mitigation: Prepare bank statements or official MoHE sponsorship letter well in advance
Check gov.uk/student-visa/money for the most current monthly figures
If MoHE covers living costs, obtain an official letter confirming the monthly allowance amount

details: From 15 July 2025, Student Route decisions are issued as a digital eVisa via your UKVI account — not as a physical sticker (vignette) in your passport
Create a UKVI account and link your eVisa before travel
Share your eVisa with airlines and employers using the online share code service at gov.uk/view-prove-immigration-status
Physical BRP cards are no longer issued for Student Route grants
controls: Register for a UKVI account immediately after visa grant
Save your share code before each international trip
Report any issues with your eVisa to UKVI before travel

details: Some nationalities are required to register with the UK police within 7 days of arriving
Check your visa conditions — if required, it will be stated on your entry clearance/eVisa
Registration is done at the Overseas Visitor Records Office (OVRO) at the local police station
Omani nationals should verify at the time of visa grant whether this condition applies
mitigation: Check your visa conditions immediately upon receipt
If required, register within 7 days of arrival — do not delay

details: You must study at the institution and on the course specified in your CAS
Sponsor monitors attendance and must report significant absences to UKVI
If you withdraw from or are excluded from your course, your sponsor must notify UKVI and your visa may be curtailed to 60 days
Changing course level downward (e.g., master's to bachelor's) triggers sponsor reporting duties

commonViolations.consequence: Sponsor reports to UKVI, visa curtailed to 60 days
UKVI notification, leave curtailed, overstay risk

UKVI_Contact_Centre.description: For visa application queries and account issues

links.title: Financial Evidence Guidance — GOV.UK
links.description: Detailed rules on acceptable financial evidence

links.title: Register of Licensed Student Sponsors
links.description: Check your university's sponsor status and track record rating

links.title: UKCISA — Student Route Eligibility
links.description: Plain-English guide to Student Route requirements

complianceNotes: MoHE-sponsored students must obtain MoHE written approval before transferring to a different UK institution
Any change in enrolment status must be reported to both your sponsor and MoHE
Financial requirements are reviewed annually — check gov.uk for current figures before applying
Your sponsor's 'track record' status determines whether you can work 20 hrs/week — verify before accepting employment

documents.title: UK Student Route — Work Rights and Restrictions
documents.summary: Students on the Student Route may work during term time within strict hour limits and without restriction during vacations. Work rights depend on your sponsor's UKVI 'track record' status and your course level. Certain types of work are always prohibited.

appliesTo: Omani students on Student Route visa
scope.failureImpact: Visa curtailment, removal from UK, scholarship revocation, future visa refusals

details: Degree level or above (e.g., bachelor's, master's, PhD) at a sponsor with 'track record' status: maximum 20 hours per week during term
Below degree level (e.g., A-levels, foundation): maximum 10 hours per week during term
If your sponsor does NOT have 'track record' status: you may have no right to work during term — check your visa conditions
The week runs Monday to Sunday; any combination of jobs must not exceed your weekly limit
You may not work before your course start date

commonViolations.text: Working more than 20 hours/week during term time
commonViolations.consequence: Breach of visa conditions, visa curtailment, possible removal

commonViolations.text: Working for a sponsor that does not have track record status when your visa only permits work at a track-record sponsor

mitigation: Verify your sponsor's track record status on the UKVI register before accepting any job
Keep payslips and timesheets to demonstrate compliance if audited
Consult your International Student Support team before accepting any employment

details: During official term vacations: no weekly hour limit — you may work full time
Also applies: before your course starts (from visa grant), and during any study break authorized by your sponsor
After your course ends: you enter a grace period (see UK-GRACE-PERIOD-2026) — you may still work if permitted by your visa conditions
Self-employment and running your own business are not permitted on the Student Route
Work as a professional sportsperson (including a sports coach) is not permitted
Work as a professional entertainer is not permitted
Unpaid internships that constitute work for immigration purposes may still count toward your hours limit
Volunteering for a charity is generally allowed and does not count toward the hours limit, but do not displace paid workers
failureModes: Freelancing or contracting through a personal company
Accepting unpaid internships that exceed your hours allowance

details: MoHE scholarship terms may restrict or prohibit employment — check your scholarship contract
Any employment income must be reported to MoHE annually
Obtaining a National Insurance (NI) number is required for employment; apply through the UK government (gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number)

links.title: Working during your Student visa — GOV.UK
links.description: Official guidance on work rights for students

links.title: Apply for a National Insurance Number
links.description: Required before starting any paid employment in the UK

documents.title: UK Graduate Route (Post-Study Work Visa)
documents.summary: The Graduate Route allows eligible graduates of UK universities to remain in the UK to work or look for work after completing their studies. It does not require employer sponsorship. Duration is 2 years for bachelor's and master's graduates (reducing to 18 months from 1 January 2027) and 3 years for PhD graduates.

appliesTo: Omani students who have completed a UK degree
scope.failureImpact: Unable to remain legally in UK; MoHE return obligation triggers if application not planned properly

timeline.T-0: Apply before current Student visa expires
timeline.After course completion confirmed: Sponsor notifies Home Office; application window opens

details: Must have completed an eligible UK bachelor's, master's, or doctoral degree at a licensed Student sponsor
Must apply before your Student Route visa expires
Your sponsor must have notified the Home Office that you successfully completed your course
Must be in the UK when you apply
No minimum salary or job offer required
Bachelor's or master's graduates: 2 years (IMPORTANT: reducing to 18 months for applications made on or after 1 January 2027)
PhD or doctoral graduates: 3 years (unchanged)
CRITICAL for MoHE scholars: Your scholarship contract requires return to Oman within 60 days of programme completion — applying for the Graduate Route does NOT override this MoHE obligation
You must obtain prior MoHE written approval before applying for the Graduate Route; failure to return may trigger full repayment obligations
failureModes: Applying for Graduate Route without MoHE approval and then being required to repay scholarship funds
Relying on 2-year grant without awareness of the January 2027 reduction to 18 months
mitigation: Contact MoHE Scholarship Office 6 months before graduation to discuss post-completion options
Get MoHE approval in writing before filing Graduate Route application
Check gov.uk/graduate-visa for the latest grant duration before applying

details: Can work in any role, at any skill level — no restriction on occupation
Can be self-employed or run a business (unlike the Student Route)
No weekly hour limit
Can switch to Skilled Worker route if employer offers sponsorship

links.title: UKCISA — Working After Studies
links.description: Guidance on Graduate Route and other post-study options

complianceNotes: MoHE approval is required before applying — do not assume the Graduate Route is compatible with your scholarship contract without written MoHE confirmation
The Graduate Route does not lead directly to settlement (Indefinite Leave to Remain) — further applications are required
Tax on UK employment income is mandatory; register with HMRC (gov.uk/register-for-self-assessment if self-employed)

documents.title: Academic Technology Approval Scheme (ATAS)
documents.summary: ATAS clearance is required before studying or researching in certain sensitive subject areas at UK universities. It is issued by the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) and must be obtained before your visa can be granted and before starting the relevant course. New applicant guidance was released 8 January 2025.

appliesTo: Postgraduate research students in sensitive STEM fields
Some taught master's students in sensitive fields
scope.failureImpact: Visa refusal, inability to start course, scholarship interruption

details: Required for students from non-exempt countries studying certain subjects at postgraduate level — primarily STEM, dual-use, and defence-sensitive fields
Your university will provide a CAH3 code and confirm whether ATAS is needed for your specific course
Omani nationals are not on the exempt list (exempt list includes EU nationals and a small number of others) — you must apply if your course requires it
Need a separate ATAS certificate for each institution and each programme of study
Apply online via the ATAS portal: academic-technology-approval.service.gov.uk
Certificate is valid for 6 months from issue date for the purpose of a visa application
Must apply early — processing can take several weeks; allow at least 4-6 weeks before your visa application
If you change institution or course, you need a new ATAS certificate
failureModes: Applying too late and missing visa application window
Changing research topic substantially without obtaining new ATAS clearance
controls: Ask your admissions team whether your course requires ATAS before accepting your offer
Apply for ATAS as soon as you receive your CAH3 code — do not wait for CAS
Build at least 6 weeks for ATAS processing into your pre-arrival timeline

links.title: ATAS — GOV.UK
links.description: Official ATAS guidance, subject lists, and application portal

links.title: Find out if you need an ATAS certificate — GOV.UK

documents.title: Maintaining Student Route Status — UK
documents.summary: Maintaining valid Student Route immigration status in the UK requires active compliance with course attendance, timely reporting of personal information changes, and understanding grace periods. Failure to maintain status can result in curtailment of leave to 60 days.

appliesTo: All Omani students on Student Route visa in UK
scope.failureImpact: Leave curtailed to 60 days, removal proceedings, future visa refusal, scholarship termination

timeline.Within 7 days of arrival: Register address with sponsor; police registration if required by visa
timeline.Before travel abroad: Confirm eVisa is accessible; no specific travel signature needed (unlike F-1 I-20)
timeline.60 days before leave expiry: Apply to extend if course continues beyond current visa
timeline.Post-completion: 60-day grace period to depart, switch status, or apply for Graduate Route

details: Attend all scheduled lectures, seminars, tutorials, and supervisor meetings as required by your sponsor
Your sponsor is legally required to monitor your attendance and report significant unauthorized absences to UKVI
If you withdraw or are excluded, your sponsor must report this to UKVI — your visa may be curtailed to 60 days from the report date
Postgraduate research students (dissertations, theses) are still monitored throughout their research period
mitigation: Notify your department and International Student Support team of any medical or personal issues before missing contact points
Keep a record of authorized absences and supervisor correspondence

details: Report your UK address to your sponsor within 7 days of arrival or moving
Address changes must be updated in your university student record system
Name or passport changes must be reported to your sponsor and UKVI promptly
Update your UKVI account with any changes to personal details
If your course continues beyond your current visa expiry, you must apply to extend before the current visa expires
Apply online via UKVI; you will need a new CAS from your sponsor
There is no automatic extension — do not let your leave expire
MoHE approval may be required for an extension if it extends total program duration beyond the original scholarship term
failureModes: Allowing leave to expire before extension is granted — unlawful presence begins
Applying without a valid CAS — application will be refused

details: Upon successful completion of your course, you have a short period (typically until your visa expiry date, or the period stated on your visa) to remain in the UK
Use this time to apply for the Graduate Route, switch to another visa, or depart
Do NOT confuse UK visa expiry with course end date — check your eVisa for your exact leave expiry date

International_Student_Support.title: Immigration and compliance advice
International_Student_Support.description: Available at all UKVI-licensed universities — check your university website for contact details

links.title: Protecting your Student status — UKCISA
links.description: Practical guide to avoiding status violations

documents.title: Healthcare in the UK — NHS and Immigration Health Surcharge
documents.summary: Most Student Route visa holders access UK healthcare through the National Health Service (NHS) after paying the Immigration Health Surcharge (IHS) as part of their visa application. Unlike the US, you are not required to purchase private insurance — the IHS covers most NHS treatment. Some services carry charges.

appliesTo: All Omani students on Student Route visa in UK
scope.failureImpact: Unexpected medical costs if IHS not paid; visa refusal if IHS payment incomplete

details: Paid upfront as part of your visa application — you cannot apply without paying it
Rate for students is currently lower than the standard rate — check gov.uk/healthcare-immigration-application/pay for the current figure before applying (rates are updated periodically)
Covers most NHS hospital and GP treatment at the same cost as UK residents
IHS does not cover: dental treatment (except emergency), optician services, and some specialist treatments
MoHE-sponsored students should check whether MoHE reimburses IHS costs — obtain written confirmation before applying
controls: Pay IHS in full as part of the visa application — partial payment causes refusal
Keep your IHS payment receipt — you may need it for NHS registration
Check gov.uk for the current IHS rate; it changes periodically

details: Register with a local GP (General Practitioner) as soon as possible after arrival — this is your primary care doctor
Use the NHS 'Find a GP' service (nhs.uk/service-search/find-a-gp) to find a surgery accepting new patients near your campus
Emergency treatment is available at A&E (Accident and Emergency) departments at NHS hospitals — call 999 for life-threatening emergencies
Call 111 (free) for urgent non-emergency medical advice, 24/7
Mental health support is available through the NHS — ask your GP for a referral
Most UK universities provide free, confidential counselling services for students
Samaritans: 116 123 (free, 24/7, anonymous) — emotional support and crisis listening service
Crisis text line: Text SHOUT to 85258 (UK, free, 24/7)

NHS_111.name: NHS 111
NHS_111.title: Urgent non-emergency medical advice
NHS_111.description: Free, 24/7. Use for urgent illness or injury that is not life-threatening

Emergency_Services.name: Emergency Services
Emergency_Services.description: Police, fire, ambulance — life-threatening emergencies only

Samaritans.name: Samaritans
Samaritans.title: Emotional support and crisis listening
Samaritans.description: Free, 24/7, anonymous — for anyone struggling or in distress

links.title: Immigration Health Surcharge — GOV.UK
links.description: Pay IHS and check current rates

links.title: Find a GP — NHS
links.description: Find a GP surgery near your university

complianceNotes: Do not confuse NHS (UK) with OSHC (Australia) or US private insurance — the IHS payment replaces the need for private insurance in most cases
Dental and optical care typically require separate private payment or private insurance — budget accordingly

documents.title: Cultural Adaptation and Islamic Accommodations — UK
documents.summary: The UK has a large and established Muslim community. Most UK universities have prayer facilities, halal food options, and student Islamic societies. Understanding UK academic and social norms will support academic success and wellbeing.

scope.failureImpact: Social isolation, academic difficulties, wellbeing challenges

details: Most UK universities have a multi-faith prayer room or Islamic prayer hall — check your university's Student Union or Faith Centre
Islamic Societies (IsocS) are active at virtually all UK universities — join early for community and practical support
Halal-certified food is widely available in UK cities; many university canteens label halal options or have dedicated sections
Ramadan accommodations (exam rescheduling, iftar events) are available — register with your department early in the semester
Friday Jumu'ah prayers: many universities allow short breaks around Friday prayer times — check with your department
controls: Contact your university's Islamic Society before arriving — most have WhatsApp groups for incoming students
Register religious observance needs at the start of term, not during exam periods

details: Independent thinking and critical analysis are highly valued — tutors expect you to question and evaluate sources, not just summarize them
Seminars and tutorials expect active participation — silence is often interpreted as disengagement, not respect
Office hours (called 'office hours' or 'drop-in sessions') are encouraged — use them; it is not seen as a sign of weakness
Plagiarism standards are strictly enforced — the UK uses Turnitin and similar tools; always cite sources, including when paraphrasing
Punctuality is expected for classes and appointments — arriving late without notice is considered disrespectful

ISOC.name: Islamic Society (IsocS)
ISOC.title: Muslim student community
ISOC.description: Available at virtually all UK universities via Student Union — search '[your university] Islamic Society'
